item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can’t believe it took me this long to find this article. As someone who disliked TekWar, this article has a way of resonating with me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if I can effectively articulate why I like this, but I do. The effect is so strange, specific, and kind of goofy. I like that kind of skip enough to +1 this one for that alone. But then the last addendum has the connection to the fan and the totally bizarre and unexpected discovery of an actual Shatner cover… it leaves me with a lot of questions, but in a way that's stimulating instead of unsatisfying. The article is very successful at making me think "What the hell happened here?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview here is well-written, but all it accomplishes is illustrating people being mean to Shatner, and the fat fuck business. Once the "fat canadian fuck" phrase is introduced it feels like it's going to build up to something, but the disgruntled fan revelation feels lacking. The way the last addenda plays out is really going to make or break the article for the reader, and for me it was the latter.

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to The Great Hippo and ihp for reviewing this in chat. This is my Jam-Con Day 2 entry.

In case anyone's not aware, the song actually does exist and rules: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ainyK6fXku0

Image is from: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:WilliamShatner_STICCON_2005-05-22.jpg and is CC-compliant.

And just in case the the theme isn't clear:

+Show spoiler
-Hide spoiler

It's a fictional (maybe) cover of a song by the band Pulp.
